title: AI-Assisted Early Detection of Diabetic Retinopathy in Rural Healthcare Settings
abstract: This proposal presents a lightweight convolutional neural network system for early-stage diabetic retinopathy detection deployable on low-resource hardware in rural clinics. The system targets regions with limited ophthalmologist access, enabling community health workers to perform preliminary screenings. Preliminary validation on 3,200 fundus images shows 91% sensitivity and 88% specificity. We request funding to expand the dataset, conduct field trials across 12 rural clinics in West Bengal, and develop a training program for healthcare workers.
methodology_full: Phase 1 (Months 1-4): Dataset expansion via partnerships with three regional hospitals. Target: 15,000 annotated fundus images across all retinopathy severity grades. Phase 2 (Months 5-8): Model refinement using transfer learning from EfficientNet-B0 baseline. Optimization for inference on Raspberry Pi 4 hardware. Phase 3 (Months 9-12): Field deployment across 12 rural clinics in Bankura and Purulia districts. Community health worker training program — 2-day workshop per clinic. Phase 4 (Months 13-15): Outcome measurement, sensitivity/specificity validation against ophthalmologist ground truth, publication.
team_full: Dr. Priya Nair — PI, Assistant Professor, Biomedical Engineering, IIT Kharagpur. 8 years experience in medical imaging.
Dr. Ayan Mukherjee — Co-PI, Ophthalmologist, SSKM Hospital Kolkata. Clinical validation lead.
Ms. Ritika Das — Research Assistant, M.Tech Computer Science. CNN architecture and deployment.
Mr. Subhash Mondal — Field Coordinator, public health background, 5 years rural healthcare NGO experience.
references_full: Gulshan V. et al. (2016). Development and Validation of a Deep Learning Algorithm for Detection of Diabetic Retinopathy. JAMA.
Ting D.S.W. et al. (2017). Development and Validation of a Deep Learning System for Diabetic Retinopathy. JAMA.
Abramoff M.D. et al. (2018). Pivotal trial of an autonomous AI-based diagnostic system for detection of diabetic retinopathy. NPJ Digital Medicine.
WHO (2023). Global Report on Diabetes. World Health Organization.

clarification_map.timeline: The 15-month timeline has been validated with our hospital partners who have confirmed data sharing agreements.
clarification_map.dataset: We have IRB approval from all three partner hospitals. Data collection begins immediately upon funding.
clarification_map.hardware: Raspberry Pi 4 was selected after benchmarking against Jetson Nano — inference time of 2.3 seconds per image is clinically acceptable.
clarification_map.overhead: The 10% overhead is standard institutional rate as per DST guidelines.

default_clarification: Thank you for your question. We are happy to provide additional details upon request. Please feel free to ask specific questions about any aspect of the proposal.
